name: Margibi County (Liberia)
description: Two equal horizontal stripes — green over red — with the Liberian national flag forming a canton in the upper hoist. In the fly half, a circular emblem shows a stylised rubber tree against a blue sky and green ground, representing the rubber plantations for which Margibi is known.
history: Margibi was created in 1985 from parts of Bong, Grand Bassa, and Montserrado counties; its flag follows the design pattern established for the original counties in 1965.

features.symbolism: Margibi as part of the Republic of Liberia
the rubber industry of Margibi (home of the Firestone plantation), shown within a green-and-blue roundel of land and sky
